message:
chore: sync beads, reference data, and legacy pointer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -97,8 +97,8 @@
 {"id":"br-2ei.4.4.1","title":"Share Attachments: bundling pipeline (hashing + copy/dedupe + inline/detach)","description":"Implement the attachment bundling pipeline for share/export.

Work:
- Decide representation for each attachment (per spec `br-2ei.4.7`):
  - inline as `data:` URI when <= threshold
  - detached marker when >= threshold
  - otherwise copy into deterministic content-addressed path layout
- Copy files into the bundle (deterministic naming) and dedupe identical content.
- Produce a machine-usable bundling index (hash -> bundle path / inline / detached marker) for downstream rewrite step.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Threshold boundaries match the legacy spec exactly (no hard-coded guessing).
- Paths are deterministic and content-addressed.
- Duplicate attachments are not recopied.
- Missing files are handled per spec (clear error or “missing marker” if legacy tolerates).
- Unit tests cover:
  - inline boundary
  - detach boundary
  - dedupe behavior
  - extension inference rules (if any)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:30.718092122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:26:49.552943955Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:26:49.552857672Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:38.917255798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:30.718092122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:39.002849166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.4.4.2","title":"Share Attachments: rewrite messages.attachments JSON in export DB","description":"Rewrite `messages.attachments` JSON in the export DB to reference bundled attachment representations.

Work:
- Parse legacy attachment JSON schema from the export DB rows.
- Rewrite per attachment to:
  - bundle-relative path
  - inline `data:` URI
  - detached marker/metadata
- Preserve legacy schema + ordering rules exactly.
- Validate that rewritten references resolve against the bundle output produced by `br-2ei.4.4.1`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON rewrite output matches the legacy schema and ordering rules (spec `br-2ei.4.7`).
- No broken references: every non-detached reference resolves to a file present in the bundle.
- Invalid/unknown JSON is handled per spec (fail-fast vs best-effort).
- Unit tests cover:
  - ordering determinism
  - mixed inline/file/detached cases
  - malformed JSON handling
- Integration test runs against a fixture DB with attachments and asserts expected rewritten JSON + hashes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:52.101600759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T14:04:44.034479958Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:04:44.034420727Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:52.101600759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:58.881861480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.4.5","title":"Share: chunking + manifest/scaffolding + viewer assets","description":"Finish share/export bundle packaging after export DB + bundled attachments are ready.

This epic is split to enable parallel implementation:
- `br-2ei.4.5.1`: chunking engine + chunk config writer (large DB path)
- `br-2ei.4.5.2`: viewer assets integration + static hosting headers
- `br-2ei.4.5.3`: manifest + scaffolding (README/_headers) + determinism glue

Notes:
- Thresholds and manifest schema MUST come from the extracted spec in `br-2ei.4.7`.
- Determinism is mandatory: stable file ordering + stable manifest ordering.

## Success Criteria
1. All child tasks are complete.
2. Bundle directory is self-contained and previewable.
3. `manifest.json` conforms to the legacy schema and is deterministically serialized.
4. Chunking works for the threshold boundary cases and is reflected in the manifest.
5. Share/export E2E (`br-2ei.9.4`) passes and produces stable hashes across two identical runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:12:55.110282213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:09.327115294Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:55:08.236817675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2ei.4.5.1","title":"Share Bundle: DB chunking engine + chunk config writer","description":"Implement the “large DB” chunking path for share/export.

Work:
- If DB size exceeds the legacy threshold (spec `br-2ei.4.7`), split DB into `chunks/`.
- Emit any required chunk config file (e.g., `mailbox.sqlite3.config.json`) with deterministic ordering.
- Ensure chunk naming and boundaries are deterministic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Threshold and output layout match the legacy spec exactly.
- Below threshold: no chunking outputs emitted (unless legacy does).
- Above threshold: bundle contains deterministic `chunks/` layout + required config.
- Unit tests cover threshold boundary cases and deterministic naming.
- Integration test verifies chunked bundle can still be queried/used by the viewer (basic sanity query).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:20.724767495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T14:05:41.458432663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:33.635936135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:20.724767495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:33.722371550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2ei.4.5.2","title":"Share Bundle: viewer assets integration + static hosting headers","description":"Integrate the static viewer assets into the share/export bundle.

Work:
- Copy/embed viewer HTML/CSS/JS assets into the bundle with legacy-correct paths.
- Emit any static-hosting metadata required by legacy (e.g., `_headers`).
- Ensure assets are deterministic (stable file ordering, stable content).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Asset set + paths match the legacy spec in `br-2ei.4.7`.
- Fetching the viewer entry page resolves all referenced assets within the bundle.
- Headers metadata is emitted exactly as expected (cache-control/content-type rules).
- Unit/integration tests validate:
  - required files present
  - expected content-type mapping or header rules","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:49.375227530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:54.689311094Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:49.375227530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:04:54.689274505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","title":"Share Bundle: manifest.json + scaffolding (README/_headers) + determinism glue","description":"Write the final bundle scaffold and `manifest.json` for share/export.

Work:
- Write `manifest.json` per legacy schema (spec `br-2ei.4.7`), including:
  - export_config
  - project_scope
  - scrub preset + version
  - DB metadata (sizes/hashes; chunks metadata when chunked)
  - attachment metadata/hashes
  - viewer/runtime flags
  - hosting hints (detected targets + signals) when present
- Ensure deterministic serialization:
  - stable key ordering
  - stable array ordering
- Write bundle README / HOW_TO_DEPLOY with “how to preview/deploy”, including legacy hosting hints:
  - Cloudflare Pages / Netlify / AWS S3 signals and the corresponding deploy steps
- Ensure `_headers`/static-hosting metadata and viewer assets are referenced correctly (and are written exactly like legacy when applicable).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `manifest.json` conforms to the legacy schema and is deterministically serialized.
- Manifest includes hashes for DB (and chunks when present) and bundled attachments per spec.
- README/HOW_TO_DEPLOY + headers scaffolding exists and is correct per legacy spec (including hosting hints/signals).
- Unit tests cover:
  - manifest determinism (serialize twice -> identical)
  - chunked vs non-chunked manifest variants
  - required fields presence
- E2E `br-2ei.9.4` can validate determinism across two exports from identical inputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:07.782474918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:42.552192062Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:16.810266743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:16.895398721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:07.782474918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:16.980221638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:17.064353973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:05:17.145302228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.4.6","title":"Share: optional signing (Ed25519) + encryption (age) + zip packaging","description":"Implement optional bundle hardening features (signing, encryption, zip packaging).

This task extends the plain directory bundle from `br-2ei.4.5`.

Work (in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/`):
- Signing (Ed25519):
  - Generate signing key if requested (or load provided key).
  - Write public key into bundle.
  - Sign `manifest.json` and include signature + metadata (algorithm, key id) per spec.
  - Provide verify flow (`--verify` or library fn) that validates signature before use.
- Encryption (age):
  - Encrypt the bundle for one or more recipients.
  - Emit `.age` output plus any required metadata.
  - Provide decrypt flow for tests.
- Zip packaging:
  - Package bundle into zip (or tar) with stable ordering + timestamps as required for determinism.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Roundtrip works:
  - Signing verify succeeds for a signed bundle.
  - Verification fails with a clear error for a tampered manifest.
  - Encryption decrypt yields byte-identical bundle output (or stable content hash if metadata differs) in test mode.
- Determinism:
  - Packaging output is deterministic given identical inputs (stable hash for the archive file).
  - File ordering inside archive is stable.
- UX:
  - CLI flags and errors are explicit (what failed, what to do).
  - Feature is optional and does not change default share behavior when disabled.
- Tests:
  - Unit tests cover sign/verify and encrypt/decrypt flows using deterministic test keys.
  - E2E `br-2ei.9.4` covers at least one signed bundle and one encrypted bundle path (can be separate runs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:13:06.191406916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:28:38.177966257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:55:08.321718960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.4.7","title":"Share spec: extract scrub rules + SQL/DDL + manifest schema + vectors","description":"Extract the exact legacy share/export behavior into a self-contained spec + test vectors.

This is *spec extraction*, not implementation. The result must be detailed enough that implementers can complete `br-2ei.4.1`..`br-2ei.4.6` without re-reading Python.

Must extract verbatim (copy exact SQL/DDL, constants, and ordering rules) from legacy Python:
- SQLite snapshot procedure:
  - WAL checkpoint details
  - backup API usage (incl. flags / sequencing)
  - any timeouts / retries / busy handling
- Project scoping:
  - tables affected
  - exact WHERE clauses / join conditions
  - any “include/exclude” semantics
- Scrub presets (standard/strict/archive):
  - exact transformations per table/column
  - deletion vs redaction rules
  - normalization rules (timestamps, ids, null handling, ordering)
- FTS + views/materialization DDL
- Export-finalize pragmas + maintenance ordering:
  - journal_mode/page_size/synchronous
  - VACUUM/ANALYZE ordering
  - any integrity checks
- Attachment bundling rules:
  - how files are copied
  - rewrite semantics for `messages.attachments` JSON
- Chunking thresholds + output naming rules
- Static hosting + deploy scaffolding:
  - hosting-hints detection signals (Cloudflare Pages / Netlify / AWS S3) and how they’re presented
  - `_headers` / other hosting metadata files (exact contents + when written)
  - README/HOW_TO_DEPLOY guidance text and any env-signal heuristics
- `manifest.json` schema:
  - required fields
  - stable ordering requirements (key order, array order)
  - versioning / compatibility expectations (even if “none”)

Deliverables:
- Add/extend a dedicated “Share/Export Spec” section in `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md` with the above as an explicit checklist.
- Add conformance fixtures for share/export (DB + expected outputs) under `crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/fixtures/share/`.
  - Include *expected hashes* (sha256) for each preset run.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The spec includes verbatim SQL/DDL and lists every table/column scrub rule for each preset.
- Spec includes the exact hosting-hints detection signals and scaffolding file contents.
- Fixtures cover at least:
  - minimal DB (smallest useful)
  - DB with attachments
  - DB requiring scrub/redaction
- For each preset, the fixture includes deterministic expected outputs (hashes + any required normalized JSON/manifest snapshots).
- A brief “How to regenerate fixtures from legacy Python” note is included (command + expected output paths).
- Implementers can complete `br-2ei.4.*` using only the spec + fixtures (no Python code consultation).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:54:17.642425426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:54:58.212246444Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:54:58.212223401Z","close_reason":"Added missing share/export fixture DDL files (expected_fts_ddl.sql, expected_views_ddl.sql); spec already present in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.4.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:54:17.642425426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
